label: Testis
quick_take: Testis is the cleanest generational-rise story in CI5 — rates roughly triple in virtually every high-income country between vol I and vol XII. The "Swiss–Alpine belt" and Chile Valdivia sit at the top in vol XII, and US Hawaiian and Asian-migrant sub-populations show dramatic late catch-ups.

findings.title: Swiss and Austrian alpine registries dominate vol XII
findings.text: Graubünden+Glarus 14.4, Berne 12.6, Lucerne 12.0, Aargau 11.4, Carinthia 11.3. The "Alpine testis belt" is a known feature; CI5 XII confirms it has strengthened further.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Swiss and Austrian alpine registries dominate vol XII

findings.title: Chile Valdivia men, vol XII: ASR 15.5 — the world's highest
findings.text: 6.4× the world median. This is counter-intuitive (testis is normally a high-income-country cancer) and worth a closer look. Possibly reflects high early-life undescended-testis prevalence + catching-up through registry improvement.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Chile Valdivia men, vol XII: ASR 15.5 — the world's highest

findings.title: US Puerto Rico: 0.45 (vol I) → 4.94 (vol XII), +998 %
findings.text: n=7→403. Large vol XII numerator, small vol I baseline; the direction is unambiguous and the final level is solid, but the % change is inflated by the small baseline.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — US Puerto Rico: 0.45 (vol I) → 4.94 (vol XII), +998 %

findings.title: Huge ethnic contrast within USA
findings.text: In vol IV, White vs Black ratio 27:1 (5.40 vs 0.20); vol XII Other-White vs Black 26:1 (8.04 vs 0.31). One of the most *widening* ethnic gradients in CI5 — the rest of the world rose but US Black testis cancer didn't.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Huge ethnic contrast within USA

findings.title: The adolescent peak (age band 5 = 20–24) is in every registry
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The adolescent peak (age band 5 = 20–24) is in every registry

findings.title: China Taiwan vol VIII shows an early-peak at age band 1 (0–4)
findings.text: probably yolk-sac tumours of infancy, a known pattern in East Asian cohorts.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — China Taiwan vol VIII shows an early-peak at age band 1 (0–4)

findings.text: The generational rise is real but some of it reflects earlier detection of subclinical seminoma; historical registries of the 1960s almost certainly under-counted.
The Chile Valdivia outlier is worth investigating — the alpine Latin-American testis-cancer literature is thin.
The widening US White-vs-Black gap is an underreported disparity.
Map the Swiss Alpine cluster against the Austrian one and test for genuine biological continuity (altitude, oxidative-stress hypotheses exist but are speculative).
